summary: Tested Emergent Google Auth integration on maligeeAi. Backend: 7 new Google-auth tests + 31 regression tests all PASS (38/38). Frontend: verified Google button, redirect to auth.emergentagent.com, cookie-based dashboard load, AuthCallback loading + redirect for invalid session_id, JWT regression, and public /share page.
critical_code_review_comments: POST /api/auth/logout only invalidates session when the cookie is present (uses request.cookies.get). If a client sends only the Authorization: Bearer <session_token> header (no cookie), the DB session row is NOT deleted, so the token continues to work. Low priority since the frontend uses cookies, but consider also parsing the Bearer token for logout robustness.
server.py is ~731 lines — approaching the recommended split threshold (~700). Consider modularizing (auth, builds, chat, share, landing routers).
test_credentials: JWT: testuser_ui_1784221710@maligee.ai / TestPass123!. Google flows use mongo-seeded sessions (see /app/auth_testing.md).
seed_data_creation: Created + auto-cleaned users (test.gauth.*@example.com, test.gui.*@example.com) and matching user_sessions rows in test_database.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Google Auth end-to-end works. Redirect URL from frontend is https://auth.emergentagent.com/?redirect=<origin>/dashboard; the provider itself 302s to /oauth/? so page.url after click ends at auth.emergentagent.com/oauth/?redirect=... — that is expected (upstream redirect), not an app bug. session_token works via both Cookie and Authorization: Bearer. Logout only clears server-side session when cookie is provided.
